# DX-WWW Launch TODO

## DX-WWW n8n Studio Foundation

- [x] 2026-06-09: add governed host-executed editor-session request batches
  for dynamic options, resource locators, mapper schemas, and credential
  metadata. The server helper now collects pending editor-session requests,
  lets host-owned adapters execute a bounded request batch, applies returned
  responses through the existing no-secret response pipeline, and advertises
  the capability in automation readiness. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-transport.test.ts .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-automation-scope.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add provider credential validation execution to the
  credential vault bridge. The vault can now resolve a credential by id, load
  its secret only inside the adapter boundary, call an injected provider
  validator, and return a redacted
  `dx.n8n-studio.credential-validation.receipt`; automation scope now marks
  the credential bridge as partial instead of metadata-only. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-credential-vault.test.ts .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-automation-scope.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add semantic generated-only node summary rendering
  across the local generated n8n connector catalog. Generic generated nodes
  now expose a non-expression `generatedNodeSummary` notice with connector
  description, source file, runtime mode, auth kind, provenance, and governed
  adapter boundaries, and the inspector renders notice fields plus parameter
  descriptions as first-class read-only UI. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add host-injected scheduler route execution for runtime
  execution-proof retries. The retry route now comes from a reusable factory:
  the public route remains credential-handoff-required by default, while
  trusted DX/Zed host code can supply credential vault, n8n API transport,
  workflow document, trigger receipt, and receipt-root inputs to run the
  bounded read-only retry executor. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-runtime-scheduler.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add injectable runtime execution-proof scheduler handoff.
  Accepted scheduler retry requests can now be paired with a caller-supplied
  credential vault, n8n API transport, workflow document, prior trigger
  receipt, and receipt root to run the bounded read-only history retry
  executor without giving the public route any credential values or transport
  ownership. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-runtime-scheduler.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add id-only scheduler POST validation for runtime
  execution-proof retries. `/api/n8n-studio/runtime-execution-proof-retry`
  now accepts the advertised scheduler POST envelope, validates
  `apiCredentialId`, the runtime proof receipt path, and bounded retry
  attempts, rejects secret-bearing request fields without echoing names or
  values, and returns a credential-handoff-required response instead of
  pretending live retry execution is available. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-runtime-scheduler.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add durable runtime execution-proof receipts and delayed
  history retry planning. The n8n Studio server bridge can now write the
  redacted execution-proof receipt under an injected receipt root, produce a
  deterministic read-only retry plan for n8n execution-history availability,
  run a bounded injected retry executor until proof is imported, advertise the
  pending proof receipt path to Zed automation consumers, and expose a
  disabled credential-handoff-required scheduler action contract through the
  Zed automation packet plus `/api/n8n-studio/runtime-execution-proof-retry`.
  Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-runtime-trigger.test.ts .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-zed-automation-bridge.test.ts .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-automation-scope.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add webhook runtime execution proof follow-through.
  Credential-vault runtime trigger submission can now pair the webhook trigger
  credential with an n8n API credential, submit the live trigger request,
  import execution history through the governed API client, and return a
  redacted proof receipt only when imported execution receipts are present.
  Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-runtime-trigger.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add governed webhook runtime trigger request contracts.
  n8n Studio now plans source-backed Webhook node trigger requests, blocks
  manual/internal triggers behind provider controls, submits webhook trigger
  URLs through credential-vault-owned secret loading, stores only redacted
  origin/status receipt metadata, and exposes trigger readiness to Zed/DX
  surfaces. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-runtime-trigger.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: bridge n8n runtime handoff through the credential vault.
  The vault can now resolve an n8n API credential by id, load the secret only
  inside the adapter boundary, submit the runtime handoff through the injected
  transport, and return only redacted receipt metadata. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-credential-vault.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add governed n8n runtime handoff receipts to the injected
  API client. `submitRuntimeHandoff` now composes sanitized workflow
  publish/update, optional activation, and optional execution-history import
  into a redacted receipt that explicitly avoids fake run/retry/stop endpoints
  and keeps live execution proof blocked behind provider triggers and imported
  receipts. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-n8n-api-client.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: overlay governed editor-session option responses into the
  visible parameter UI state. Applying a redacted dynamic-options response now
  updates selected parameter options through a small overlay helper, renders
  configured option controls, and preserves resource-locator resolved options
  without enabling live provider execution. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-transport.test.ts --json`,
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`,
  and
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-resource-locator.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: wire current workflow export into the real n8n Studio UI
  state. The Import / export panel now exposes an `Export current workflow`
  action that POSTs the live DX `WorkflowDocument` to
  `/api/n8n-studio/export`, stores only redacted status/receipt metadata, and
  keeps the exported workflow payload out of client state. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-export.test.ts --json` and
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-apply-save.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add Zed-readable n8n Studio receipt export for the
  automation packet and readiness snapshot. The bridge writer materializes
  `agents/automate-list-latest.json` and
  `n8n-studio/readiness-latest.json` under an injected receipt root, preserves
  redaction guarantees, and updates automation scope so n8n runtime execution
  is partial rather than only planned. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-zed-automation-bridge.test.ts --json`
  and
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-automation-scope.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add governed n8n workflow publish handoff through the
  injected n8n API client. The client now supports `POST /workflows`,
  `PUT /workflows/{id}`, and explicit `POST /workflows/{id}/activate`
  requests, builds the stricter n8n create/update body from the current DX
  workflow document, strips secret-like fields before transport, and returns a
  redacted publish receipt that does not claim execution proof. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-n8n-api-client.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-owned current workflow JSON export for edited
  DX n8n Studio state. `/api/n8n-studio/export` now supports POST payloads
  containing the current `WorkflowDocument`, sanitizes secret-like parameter
  and credential fields, and returns n8n-shaped workflow JSON plus export
  receipt metadata instead of only exporting the seeded boot workflow. Guarded
  by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-export.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: expose n8n Studio readiness as a Zed automation-list bridge
  packet. Added a source-only `dx.agents.zed.automation_list.v1` projection
  for the n8n Studio editor-session row with runtime unavailable, no
  secret-bearing actions, redacted receipt references, and a GET route at
  `/api/n8n-studio/zed-automation`. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-zed-automation-bridge.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: narrow n8n Studio readiness around DX-native
  n8n-compatible automation instead of n8n Vue editor parity. Readiness now
  exposes an explicit automation scope contract covering node metadata,
  workflow JSON authoring, connection authoring, n8n runtime execution,
  credential bridge, and dynamic option/resource-locator capability states.
  Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-automation-scope.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: expose governed editor-session request batches for DX Agents
  and Zed plugin panel consumers. `/api/n8n-studio/editor-session` now serves
  pending blocked request plans through GET with request-kind counts,
  selected-node metadata, provider-boundary flags, redaction metadata, and no
  secret values, while POST remains the governed response-batch application
  path. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-transport.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add governed editor-session credential validation request
  and response handling. Selected credentials now emit separate
  `credential-test` plans, governed validation responses can configure those
  plans with valid/invalid/unknown metadata, the runtime decoder rejects
  unsafe/live/secret-bearing validation records, and readiness projections
  expose validation request counts. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`
  and
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-transport.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: wire governed editor-session response batches into real
  Studio state and API plumbing. Added a store action, server response helper,
  runtime response-batch decoder, and `/api/n8n-studio/editor-session` POST
  route so DX-owned bridges can apply configured request-plan responses while
  rejecting unsafe/live/secret-bearing payloads and returning updated
  readiness metadata. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-transport.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: apply governed editor-session credential-list responses to
  matching request plans. Credential picker plans now accept DX-redacted,
  no-live-provider credential list responses, sanitize options down to
  id/name/type/source/redaction metadata, update selected credential id/name,
  and drop unexpected secret fields before data enters Studio state. Guarded
  by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: apply governed editor-session resource mapper schema
  responses to matching request plans. Resource mapper plans now accept
  DX-redacted, no-live-provider schema responses with sanitized field ids,
  display names, required/matching flags, types, field words, and field counts,
  dropping unexpected provider fields before they reach Studio state. Guarded
  by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: apply governed editor-session resource-locator search
  responses to matching request plans. Resource locator plans now preserve
  sanitized result options plus the resolved query and pagination cursor from
  DX-redacted, no-live-provider transport responses, giving future picker UI
  and DX Agents bridge consumers a real configured list/search state without
  exposing secret fields. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: apply governed editor-session dynamic option transport
  responses to matching request plans. The adapter now accepts DX-redacted,
  no-live-provider response payloads, strips unexpected option fields before
  storing resolved options, tracks fulfilled request count, and exposes that
  count through the readiness bridge while unresolved editor-session requests
  remain blocked. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: render generated trigger nodes with explicit source-only
  trigger configuration boundaries. Generated trigger records such as Acuity
  Scheduling Trigger now expose a non-expression `triggerConfiguration`
  notice alongside credential pickers, making the inspector and DX Agents
  bridge honest about trigger metadata being known while detailed event fields
  and live webhook execution remain blocked behind source-backed trigger
  adapters. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: prefer generated executable action metadata for generic
  generated node operation selectors and catalog operation summaries when
  local n8n generated records expose `actions`. Nodes such as Chargebee now
  avoid showing operator-like filter values such as `is` and `gte` as
  top-level executable operations while preserving real actions such as
  `cancel` and `delete` in generated schemas and node-creator catalog data.
  Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: preserve source-backed Discord v2 send-and-wait custom-form
  fields from n8n's local Form node helper sources. Discord `sendAndWait`

  field-based `formFields` fixed-collection children such as field type,
  dropdown/radio/checkbox options, required flags, file controls, and
  placeholder/default-value fields in isolated extraction and generated Studio

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Discord v2 send-and-wait parameter

  expose `sendAndWait`, send-to resource locators, message text, response

- [x] 2026-06-09: expand source-backed Discord v2 message operations beyond

- [x] 2026-06-09: expand source-backed Discord v2 parameter extraction to

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Discord v2 message-send parameter

- [x] 2026-06-09: preserve Notion v2 function-generated database-page search

- [x] 2026-06-09: preserve Notion v2 function-generated block builder

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Notion v2 parameter extraction and
  generated boot overlay. Notion v2 now reads local `VersionDescription.ts`
  plus shared block/database/database-page/page/user description arrays for

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: move Google Sheets nested object-spread parsing into the
  shared n8n source-description reader. The helper now handles same-source
  object spreads such as `readFilter` plus nested shared object spreads such
  as `handlingExtraData`, letting the Google Sheets adapter drop its custom
  recursive parser while preserving append/read schemas. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-description-reader.test.ts --json`
  and
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: route Gmail operation-field arrays through the shared n8n
  source-description reader. Gmail now shares version, credential, and array

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add same-source array spread support to the shared n8n
  source-description reader. OpenAI now uses the common helper for local
  spread-backed `chatFields`, `imageFields`, and `textFields`, including

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: reuse the shared n8n source-description reader for Gmail
  base metadata. Gmail now reads `versionDescription.properties` and
  credential requirements through the common helper while keeping its

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: reuse the shared n8n source-description reader for Google
  Sheets base metadata. Google Sheets now reads its leading `authentication`

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: extract shared n8n source-description reader for

  `updateDisplayOptions` merge semantics, version properties, and credential

  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Postgres v2 database operation

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Airtable v2 base/record operation

- [x] 2026-06-09: apply source-backed child parameter display rules. Nested

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Google Sheets v2 append parameter

- [x] 2026-06-09: make the DX n8n Studio store generated-catalog aware. Added

  node-creator, parameter, and import/export actions through that registry.
  Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-node-creator-actions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: wire the n8n Studio export API to generated catalog boot
  context. The export route now uses the same local generated connector boot as
  the Studio shell, reports generated metadata/catalog node counts for bridge
  consumers, and still exports only source-owned workflow JSON with
  `liveProviderExecution=false`. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-export.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: wire the n8n Studio readiness API to generated catalog boot
  data. The readiness route now uses the local generated connector catalog,
  exposes generated metadata and catalog node counts for DX Agents/Zed bridge
  consumers, and keeps live provider execution disabled. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: boot the default n8n Studio page from local generated
  connector metadata. Added a shared server boot helper that reads the local
  generated connector catalog, builds the full generated Studio catalog, merges
  generated node types with first-party source-backed registry overrides, and
  passes both generated state and registry into the client app. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: make generated-registry import preview application refresh
  real selected-node surfaces. `applyImportExportActionToStudioState` now
  accepts an injected semantic registry for preview loading, apply/save
  transitions, selected parameter creation, credential readiness, and
  editor-session readiness, allowing generated Gmail workflow previews to apply
  with real inspector state. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-apply-save.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: make n8n Studio workflow import sanitation registry-aware.
  `sanitizeImportedWorkflow`, node sanitation, and import preview creation now
  accept an injected semantic registry, so generated-registry workflows can keep
  nodes such as Gmail, preserve valid connections/pin data, and still strip
  secret-like parameters without reporting supported generated nodes as
  unknown. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-export.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: make n8n Studio node creation registry-aware for generated
  connector metadata. `createSelectedNodeParameters`, editor-session readiness,
  catalog filtering, and add-node insertion can now use an injected semantic
  registry, allowing generated catalog entries such as Gmail to become addable
  and refresh parameters, credentials, expression state, and editor-session
  readiness without falling back to fake nodes. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-node-creator-actions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: replace the n8n Studio catalog panel placeholder with a real
  source-owned node creator slice. Added query-backed catalog filtering,
  addability projection from the semantic node registry, mutation-backed
  add-node behavior for supported first-party nodes, selected-node surface
  refresh after insertion, and blocked unsupported catalog entries without fake
  workflow nodes. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-node-creator-actions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed Gmail v2 parameter extraction for n8n
  Studio. Added a local-source adapter over `nodes/Google/Gmail/v2` that reads
  the base Gmail node description plus message/label/draft/thread parameter
  arrays, preserves `multiOptions` fields, maps n8n `loadOptionsMethod`
  boundaries for labels/aliases/thread messages, parses Gmail credential
  metadata from source, and collects nested dynamic option boundaries from
  collection child fields such as `options.fromAlias`. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add the first source-owned `examples/n8n-studio`
  foundation with a modern extensionless `dx` app contract, DX Style/DX Icon
  surfaces, typed n8n catalog/workflow/parameter/credential/execution/AI/import
  receipt domains, readiness/catalog/export route handlers, and a focused
  source-guard benchmark. Live n8n execution remains explicitly blocked behind
  provider configuration, credential readiness, project scope, and receipt
  import instead of being represented as already working.

- [x] 2026-06-08: advance the n8n Studio foundation from static parameter
  fields to semantic source-owned node metadata. Added per-node registry modules
  for HTTP Request, Slack, and OpenAI, metadata-driven parameter schema
  construction with credential selects/resource locator modes/display rules, and
  workflow import sanitation that strips secrets, drops unknown nodes, cleans
  invalid connections/pin data, and regenerates imported webhook IDs. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-semantic-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add generated local connector metadata ingestion for n8n
  Studio. Added a pure adapter for
  `integrations/n8n-nodes-base/generated/dx-automations-connectors.json`, a
  deterministic deduped node-type registry over the 536 generated connector
  records, generated resource/operation/credential parameter fields, generated
  catalog projection, and server catalog response wiring with an honest
  source-missing fallback. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-generated-metadata.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add source-backed Slack message parameter extraction for n8n
  Studio. Added a narrow parameter-source adapter over
  `nodes/Slack/V2/MessageDescription.ts` that reads exported n8n parameter
  objects/arrays, resolves spread-based Slack resource locators, produces
  resource/operation/select/channel/user/message type/text/block/collection
  fields, records complex source-field rendering boundaries, and exposes those
  boundaries in the parameter inspector. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: expand source-backed Slack parameters with nested
  collection/fixed-collection child metadata. Attachments and other options now
  carry child parameter definitions extracted from local n8n source, including
  nested attachment field groups such as title/value/short, and the parameter
  inspector summarizes child fields without flattening the source boundary.
  Guarded by `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: bind nested Slack collection/fixed-collection values into
  editor-ready parameter state. Source-backed attachment values now produce
  repeatable `collectionItems`, nested fixedCollection groups keep item labels
  and field values, and the parameter inspector renders recursive source-child
  controls instead of summary-only text. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add source-owned nested parameter mutation actions for n8n
  Studio. Added immutable add/remove/update helpers for nested collection and
  fixedCollection parameter paths, selected-node schema refresh after mutations,
  store action wiring, and callback-gated collection controls that do not render
  dummy buttons without real handlers. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-collection-actions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: wire nested parameter mutation actions into the live n8n
  Studio client shell. The shell now owns editable Studio state, applies
  selected-node add/remove/update mutations through the source-owned reducer,
  and passes real handlers through the inspector into recursive parameter
  controls. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-collection-actions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add type-aware parameter value coercion for live n8n Studio
  edits. Boolean parameters now update from checkbox values, numeric
  parameters coerce finite decimal input, JSON and resource-locator payloads
  parse structured JSON input when available, and mutation callbacks carry
  typed values instead of string-only edits. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-collection-actions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add resource-locator draft state and list/search boundary
  modeling for n8n Studio. Resource-locator parameter fields now carry active
  mode, selected value, cached label/query, searchable mode metadata, and an
  explicit source-only editor-session adapter boundary for dynamic list/search
  calls; the parameter control renders mode/value controls without claiming
  live provider search. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-resource-locator.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add selected-node credential picker readiness for n8n
  Studio. Credential readiness is now derived from the selected workflow node
  and node-type credential requirements, keeps only credential ids/display
  names, exposes picker options and required/missing status, and renders
  provider-boundary markers without loading or storing secret values. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-credentials.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add import/export session readiness for n8n Studio.
  Import/export state now exposes source-only import source options, an honest
  awaiting-input default preview, sanitized imported-workflow preview summaries,
  issue actions, non-executable import boundaries, and export receipt metadata
  shared by the panel and export route. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-export.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add execution debug readiness for n8n Studio.
  Execution state now exposes a blocked manual-run attempt, per-node debug log
  rows, receipt import boundaries, no-secret redaction markers, execution
  action metadata, and readiness API counters for the DX Agents bridge without
  claiming live provider execution. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-execution-debug.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add execution receipt import for n8n Studio.
  Execution debug state can now consume a replayable execution receipt, map
  known node logs onto the workflow, preserve success/error/timing/item-count
  summaries, surface provider error messages, drop unknown-node logs, strip
  secret-like fields and field names, and keep live provider execution false.
  Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-execution-receipt-import.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add source-backed HTTP Request V3 parameter extraction for
  n8n Studio. Added a pure adapter over local
  `nodes/HttpRequest/V3/Description.ts` plus the shared response optimizer
  parameter source, preserving curl import, credential-select metadata,
  boolean display rules, hide rules, fixedCollection query/header/body fields,
  and tool-only optimize-response parameters without importing n8n runtime
  code. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add n8n public API execution-history import for n8n Studio.
  Added a read-only request plan tied to local `nodes/N8n/ExecutionDescription.ts`
  and `n8n-api-coverage.json`, plus sanitized conversion from `GET /executions`
  history payloads into governed execution receipts with counts, timings,
  provider error messages, unknown-node issues, and no raw output or secret-like
  field preservation. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-execution-history-api.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add the authenticated n8n public API client boundary for n8n
  Studio. Added an injected-transport adapter for `GET /workflows/{id}` and
  `GET /executions` that sends the API key only inside the transport request,
  returns redacted readiness metadata, sanitizes workflow import previews, and
  routes execution history through governed receipts. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-n8n-api-client.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add the credential-vault bridge for n8n Studio. Added
  redacted vault readiness, id-based n8n API credential resolution through an
  injected secret loader, default blocked readiness API metadata, and proof that
  returned Studio state does not persist secret values, secret refs, or
  secret-like field names. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-credential-vault.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add the editor-session adapter readiness layer for n8n
  Studio. Added source-owned request plans for dynamic node parameters,
  resource-locator search/list calls, and selected-node credential picker data;
  projected counts and redaction boundaries into Studio state and the readiness
  API without live provider execution. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-08: add source-owned canvas pointer and keyboard interaction
  runtime for n8n Studio. Added typed canvas actions for selection, drag, pan,
  zoom, fit, tidy, delete, select-all, and keyboard nudging; selected-node
  parameter/credential/editor-session surfaces now refresh from canvas
  selection, and the canvas UI wires real pointer/keyboard handlers instead of
  inert controls. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-canvas-interactions.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-owned canvas edge handling for n8n Studio. Added
  validated source/target handle connection creation, duplicate and self-loop
  rejection, dynamic edge rendering, draggable reconnect handles, endpoint
  validation for source and target reconnects, and focused component wiring
  guards. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-canvas-edges.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-owned import apply/save flows for n8n Studio.
  Added a typed import action reducer that loads sanitized previews without
  mutating the current workflow, blocks previews with blocker sanitation issues,
  applies clean previews into the real editor document/canvas/inspector state,
  refreshes pinned data, execution readiness, export receipts, and credential
  readiness, records source-only draft save metadata, and adds first-party
  Manual Trigger metadata for common n8n imports. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-import-apply-save.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-backed OpenAI parameter extraction and dynamic
  option boundaries for n8n Studio. Added a pure adapter over local
  `nodes/OpenAi/ChatDescription.ts`, `TextDescription.ts`, and
  `ImageDescription.ts`, expanded the default OpenAI metadata to real chat,
  text-edit, and image-generation parameter shapes, modeled model pickers as
  source-only dynamic option requests, and projected those requests into
  editor-session readiness without live provider calls. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-source-parameters.test.ts --json`
  and
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-editor-session-adapter.test.ts --json`.

- [x] 2026-06-09: add source-owned expression editor state for n8n Studio.
  Added n8n expression parsing for selected-node parameter fields, source-only
  preview boundaries, credential/env secret-reference diagnostics, recursive
  field annotations, and a real expression editor panel driven by Studio state
  instead of fixed demo text. Canvas selection, import apply, and selected-node
  parameter mutations now refresh expression-editor state with the other
  inspector surfaces. Guarded by
  `dx run --test .\benchmarks\n8n-studio-expression-editor.test.ts --json`.

- [ ] Continue the n8n Studio lane by generating semantic node type
  descriptions beyond the generated connector resource/operation shell, then
  expanding source-backed parameter extraction beyond HTTP Request, Slack, and
  OpenAI, implementing real editor-session transport behind the readiness

## Architecture Scope Reset

- [x] 2026-05-23: remove external framework debugging tool clone targets and
  Turbopack runtime/build adoption from the active DX-WWW completion target.
  DX-WWW is a DX-owned WWW framework with Next-familiar authoring. External
  framework debugging tool clone targets are out of scope. Turbopack runtime/build
  adoption is out of scope. Keep DX dev feedback, hot reload, the basic overlay
  at `/_dx/feedback`, diagnostics/code frames, App Router support, route
  graph/status, Forge-first/no-node_modules behavior, and Turbopack
  reference/provenance only. Runtime/build adoption is not a DX-WWW goal, and
  Turbopack does not power `dx build` or `dx dev`.

- [x] 2026-05-23: remove template/readiness status fields that kept Turbopack
  adoption/power and external framework parity as active contract vocabulary. The
  default template architecture contract now reports `dx_source_build=true`,
  external bundler runtime execution/requirement false, and
  `next_familiar_authoring=true`; the launch readiness Zed handoff summary
  consumes and prints those DX-owned fields. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\default-www-template-contract.test.ts`, the scope
  removal guard, and touched-file rustfmt. Remaining proof: focused Cargo after
  the shared Rust queue clears.

- [x] 2026-05-23: remove active external framework parity field names from App Router
  request-props, navigation control-flow, metadata, and render-plan contracts.
  These surfaces now report DX-owned source contract fields and
  external-runtime-required/executed false markers while preserving safe
  request props, redirect/notFound metadata, source-owned metadata extraction,
  and render-plan production blockers. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-router-source-owned-vocabulary.test.cjs`
  plus focused request-props, navigation, metadata, and render-plan guards.
  Remaining proof: focused Cargo after the shared Rust queue clears.

## Route Handler Response Status Aliases

- [x] 2026-05-23: support simple local numeric status aliases in safe
  App Router route-handler responses, including `Response.json(..., { status:
  accepted })` and `new Response(..., { status: accepted })` when `accepted`
  is declared as a literal numeric local. This keeps route execution
  source-owned and leaves arbitrary expression evaluation out of scope. Guarded
  with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\app-api-route-handler-extensions.test.mjs` and
  touched-file rustfmt. Remaining proof: focused `dx-www-compiler` behavior
  test after the shared Rust queue clears, plus dev/build route proof when
  approved.

## Next-Familiar Fixture Root Coverage

- [x] 2026-05-23: make the `dx build` Next-familiar fixture writer use the
  same root/extension shape as the migration evidence path for page fixtures:

  longer assume root `app/`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-build-next-familiar-fixtures-contract.test.cjs`
  and touched-file rustfmt. Remaining proof: run a real `dx build` fixture
  after the shared build tree is stable enough to execute safely.

## Source Resolver Package Export Boundaries

- [x] 2026-05-23: keep package self-reference subpaths behind explicit
  `package.json` `exports`. `dx build` no longer lets the generic `name/*`
  source fallback link unexported local files when exports are present; blocked
  or unexported subpaths are recorded as package-export adapter boundaries
  without requiring `node_modules`. Guarded with red/green
  `node --test .\benchmarks\source-resolver-package-export-boundary.test.cjs`,
  the existing source resolver guard, and touched-file rustfmt. Remaining
  proof: run the focused `source_resolver_compat` Cargo fixture after the
  shared Cargo/rustc queue clears.

## App Router Navigation RedirectType Control Flow

- [x] 2026-05-23: accept safe Next-familiar redirect type arguments in the
  source-owned App Router navigation control-flow scanner: `"push"`,

  still ignores comments/string literals and leaves dynamic redirect
  destinations as adapter-boundary work. Guarded with red/green

  alternate-target attempt did not finish before timeout.

## Route Handler NextRequest URL Properties

## App Router Catch-All Semantics

- [x] 2026-05-23: preserve Next-familiar required versus optional catch-all

  remains optional, and both `RoutePattern` plus the exported `RouteMatcher`

  build proof.

## Rust Router Request Normalization

## dx-style Functional Utility CSS-First Slice

- [x] 2026-05-23: implemented the next coherent Tailwind v4.3 utility/directive
  slice without importing Tailwind. Flat `@utility` rules now support common

- [x] AI extended route opt-in interpreter gate, 2026-05-23: the safe Rust

- [x] DX Build deploy-adapter route-handler proof hardening, 2026-05-23: the

- [x] InstantDB route-handler configured POST dry-run, 2026-05-23: `/api/instant`

- [x] Package receipt interaction vocabulary cleanup, 2026-05-23: checked-in

- [x] AI extended stream route provider-boundary hardening, 2026-05-23: the

- [x] dx-style Tailwind v4.3 gap matrix, 2026-05-23: verified the current

  config/plugin boundaries, and governed live Tailwind output comparison remain

  `node --test .\benchmarks\dx-style-v43-gap-matrix.test.ts` and green

The latest red/green fixture covers TSX `app/page.tsx` transformed into `tsx-component-runtime`, nested imported components, dependency runtime export bindings, a local TS helper transformed into `typescript-helper-runtime`, a simple TSX `Badge` transformed into `tsx-leaf-runtime`, a simple imported `Hero` transformed into `tsx-component-runtime`, flattened and minified token CSS imports with unreachable-class pruning metadata, a public SVG asset, fallback `index.html`, `index.dxpk`, `page-graph.json`, `route-unit.json`, an executable ESM route-shell chunk importing `dxRouteEntryModule`, source-module chunks, graph nodes/edges, canonical receipts, consumer snapshot, and Zed handoff. Verification: `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --test source_build_engine`, `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli module_runtime_transform --lib`, `cargo test -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli module_tsx_component_runtime --lib`, focused CSS optimizer tests, targeted rustfmt, and the Node graph consumer benchmark. Checkpoint before the later CSS summary/graph/Zed handoff slices: 98/100 source-ready. Remaining blockers: governed installed-binary smoke, live runtime/hydration proof, broader TSX/runtime lowering beyond the simple component fixture, broad CSS transform compatibility, and full bundler parity.

- [x] TSX class-list and class-call prop lowering: source-owned component previews now lower bounded React class-list expressions using static arrays, truthy `condition && "class"` entries, template prop bindings, and `.filter(Boolean).join(" ")`, plus bounded `cn(...)` / `clsx(...)` calls for literal, prop-bound, template, logical, and ternary arguments. - [x] TSX ordered child-tree rendering: the TSX lowering surface now records `parent_index` and ordered `child_nodes` for JSX elements, and the App Router source renderer uses that tree to render nested intrinsic JSX plus nested source-owned component previews instead of flattening JSX elements into sibling-only static snapshots. This improves normal `app/page.tsx` and local component composition without claiming arbitrary React execution. Verification: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024` on touched TSX runtime files, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` with the existing unrelated `THREE_SCENE_OFFICIAL_PACKAGE_NAME` warning. Exact next action: add safe evaluation for common expression patterns such as simple ternaries, literal-array `.map(...)`, and `props.className` merges.

- [x] TSX static template prop interpolation: source-owned component return previews now resolve bounded template literals such as `` `card ${className}`.trim() `` and `` `Hello ${props.name}` `` when every interpolation is either a literal or a resolved caller prop binding. This makes common className merge and text interpolation patterns render in the TSX static preview without executing arbitrary JavaScript. Verification: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `rustfmt --edition 2024` on the TSX source renderer, and `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli` with the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning. Exact next action: add safe ternary/class-list lowering for common `variant === "x" ? "a" : "b"` and `["a", active && "b"].filter(Boolean).join(" ")` patterns.

- [x] TSX static conditional prop branches: the source renderer now lowers bounded ternaries such as `active ? "enabled" : "disabled"` and `` variant === "primary" ? `button ${variant}` : "button" `` when the condition and branch outputs can be resolved from literals or caller prop bindings. This covers a common React className/text branch without executing general JavaScript. Verification: red/green `dx run --test .\benchmarks\public-framework-contract.test.ts`, `cargo check -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli --tests`, and targeted `cargo test -q -p dx-www --no-default-features --features cli resolves_static_conditional_prop_branch --lib` with the existing unrelated Three Scene dead-code warning. Exact next action: add safe class-list lowering for `active && "class"` plus `.filter(Boolean).join(" ")`.

- [x] Launch route correction: the launch starter now treats `app/page.tsx` and `/` as the primary TSX dashboard entrypoint, keeps `app/page.tsx` as the Studio/docs alias, and keeps legacy page formats out of the current public authoring path.
- [x] TSX default runtime correction: `dx-www dev` now serves generated launch routes through a scoped TSX App Router launch responder when `app/page.tsx` / `app/page.tsx` imports `LaunchShell`, so `/` and `/launch` no longer leak literal `{children}` while still preserving honest adapter-boundary states.
- [x] Public `.tsx` framework contract surfaced in CLI/docs: `dx style build|watch|check`, `dx imports sync|check`, `dx check web-perf`, and `dx deploy vercel` now have source-owned command paths and receipt/manifest contracts.
- [x] Make the PostCSS replacement contract explicit in `dx style` receipts: build/check now publish scan roots, extensions, class/className extraction patterns, `styles/theme.dx.css`, `styles/app.generated.css`, no PostCSS adapter dependency, no Tailwind dependency, and no template-local `node_modules` requirement.
- [x] Block legacy CSS tool configs in `dx style check`: root-level `postcss.config.*` and `tailwind.config.*` files now produce `legacy_css_tooling_findings` so the official template cannot silently reintroduce PostCSS/Tailwind while claiming dx-style replacement status.
- [x] Block legacy CSS package dependencies in `dx style check`: `package.json` entries for `tailwindcss`, `postcss`, `autoprefixer`, and `@tailwindcss/postcss` now produce `legacy_css_dependency_findings` so the official template keeps the no-dependency-black-hole promise.
- [x] Block legacy CSS lockfile residue in `dx style check`: `package-lock.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `yarn.lock`, `bun.lock`, and `bun.lockb` are scanned for Tailwind/PostCSS packages and report `legacy_css_lockfile_findings` if stale dependency entries remain.
- [x] Tokenize the launch-template runtime CSS bridge: `examples/template/runtime-assets/launch-runtime.css` now aliases DX theme tokens through `--dx-*` variables and removes hardcoded hex/rgb color literals from the runtime-safe CSS surface.

- [x] Tighten `dx style check` into a real public-template gate: missing required Vercel-like tokens, stale generated CSS, unused generated classes, hardcoded colors, and Tailwind leakage now block the style check instead of landing as advisory metadata.
- [x] Add a normal CSS renderer to `dx-www/src/build/style.rs`: `StyleCompiler::compile_normal_css` and `STYLE_PUBLIC_OUTPUT_MODE` now make normal generated CSS the explicit public compiler path while DXC1 remains deprecated/internal compatibility.
- [x] Add a TSX class scanner entry point to `dx-www/src/parser/style.rs`: `STYLE_CLASS_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` and `extract_class_attribute_tokens` now extract static `className`/`class` tokens for the dx-style PostCSS replacement path without claiming dynamic expression parity yet.
- [x] Extend the class scanner contract to static TSX expression strings: parser and CLI extraction now cover `className={\`...\`}`, `className={"..."}`, `className={'...'}`, and matching `class={...}` forms while leaving arbitrary JavaScript class expression parsing as a known gap.
- [x] Extend class scanning to static helper-call strings: parser and CLI extraction now cover quoted tokens in `cn(...)`, `cx(...)`, `clsx(...)`, `classNames(...)`, and `cva(...)` calls for common TSX class composition while still leaving arbitrary JavaScript branch evaluation out of scope.
- [x] Keep scanner output concrete: dynamic template placeholders such as `dx-${variant}` are now filtered out before generated CSS selector discovery, while neighboring static class tokens still scan normally.
- [x] Wire `dx style build` to the dx-style utility engine: build/check now report scanned static class tokens separately from generated `dx-*` template classes and generated utility classes, and supported static utilities are emitted as normal CSS instead of placeholder selectors.
- [x] Wire the same dx-style public-template signals into core `dx check` through a dedicated `dx-style` section for required tokens, generated CSS freshness, hardcoded colors, Tailwind leakage, and unused generated selectors.
- [x] Add the dx-style launch compatibility matrix and public-doc guard: `related-crates/style/TAILWIND_COMPATIBILITY.md` now records layout, flex, grid, spacing, sizing, typography, colors, borders, radius, shadows, opacity, transforms, transitions, animation, filters, responsive variants, dark mode, hover/focus/active, data/aria/group/peer variants, arbitrary values, negative values, important modifiers, and container queries without claiming total Tailwind parity.
- [x] Implement the first post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: important modifiers now normalize `!p-4`, `hover:!bg-blue-500`, and encoded child utilities such as `!space-x-4` into generated declarations with `!important`, while preserving authored selectors.
- [x] Implement the second post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: `data-open:*`, `aria-expanded:*`, and `data-[state=open]:*` now map to attribute selectors through guarded state-wrapper helpers without promoting full Tailwind variant grammar.
- [x] Implement the third post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: simple named group/peer variants such as `group-hover/card:*`, `group-focus/nav:*`, and `peer-checked/published:*` now map to named selectors through guarded state-wrapper helpers.
- [x] Implement the fourth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: Tailwind-style container min-width variants such as `@sm:*`, `@lg:hover:*`, and `@[475px]:*` now map to normal `@container (min-width: ...)` CSS through guarded state-wrapper helpers.
- [x] Implement the fifth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: common transition property classes plus `duration-*`, `delay-*`, `ease-*`, and arbitrary transition time/easing values now generate normal CSS through guarded utility helpers.
- [x] Implement the sixth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: Tailwind-style `animate-spin`, `animate-ping`, `animate-pulse`, `animate-bounce`, `animate-none`, and arbitrary `animate-[...]` values now generate normal CSS/keyframes through guarded animation helpers.
- [x] Implement the seventh post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: safe arbitrary group/peer selector variants such as `group-[.is-open]:*` and `peer-[data-state=checked]:*` now map to group/peer selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the eighth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: named container variants such as `@lg/main:*` and max-width container variants such as `@max-md:*` / `@max-[960px]:*` now map to normal `@container` CSS through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the ninth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: transition property aliases such as `transition-[height,opacity]` / `transition-(--dx-transition-property)` and transition behavior utilities `transition-discrete` / `transition-normal` now generate normal CSS through guarded utility helpers.
- [x] Implement the tenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: animation CSS variable aliases such as `animate-(--dx-animation-enter)` now generate normal CSS through guarded animation helpers.
- [x] Implement the eleventh post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: safe generic arbitrary selector variants such as `[&>svg]:*` and `[&:nth-child(3)]:*` now map to selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the twelfth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: text flow and line-clamp utilities such as `line-clamp-3`, `line-clamp-none`, `line-clamp-[7]`, `hyphens-auto`, `text-balance`, `text-ellipsis`, and `text-clip` now generate normal CSS through guarded utility helpers.
- [x] Implement the thirteenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: responsive media variants such as `max-md:*`, `min-[475px]:*`, `max-[960px]:*`, `motion-safe:*`, `motion-reduce:*`, `portrait:*`, `landscape:*`, and `print:*` now map to normal nested CSS through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the fourteenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: feature-query variants such as `supports-[display:grid]:*`, `supports-[backdrop-filter:blur(0)]:*`, and `not-supports-[display:grid]:*` now map to safe `@supports` wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the fifteenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: named arbitrary group/peer selector variants such as `group-[.is-open]/card:*` and `peer-[data-state=checked]/published:*` now map to named selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the sixteenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: `:has()` selector variants such as `has-checked:*`, `has-[img]:*`, `group-has-[a]/card:*`, and `peer-has-[input:checked]/published:*` now map to safe state/arbitrary selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the seventeenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: `:not()` selector variants such as `not-hover:*`, `not-[.is-open]:*`, `group-not-hover/card:*`, and `peer-not-checked/published:*` now map to safe state/arbitrary selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the eighteenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: child selector variants `*:*` and `**:*` now map to direct-child and descendant selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the nineteenth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: `in-*` ancestor variants such as `in-hover:*` and `in-[.is-open]:*` now map to safe `:where(...)` ancestor wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the twentieth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: nth structural variants such as `nth-3:*` and `nth-[2n+1]:*` now map to safe `:nth-child(...)` selector wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the twenty-first post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: extended nth structural variants such as `nth-last-2:*`, `nth-of-type-[3n+1]:*`, and `nth-last-of-type-2:*` now map to safe structural pseudo-class wrappers through guarded state helpers.
- [x] Implement the twenty-second post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: structural pseudo-class variants such as `only:*`, `first-of-type:*`, `last-of-type:*`, and `only-of-type:*` now map through default state tokens and shared state selector guards.
- [x] Implement the twenty-third post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: arbitrary descendant and sibling selector variants such as `[&_p]:*`, `[&+*]:*`, `[&~*]:*`, and escaped literal underscores such as `[&_.file\_name]:*` now map through guarded arbitrary selector decoding and wrappers.
- [x] Implement the twenty-fourth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: arbitrary at-rule variants such as `[@media_(any-hover:hover)]:*`, `[@supports_(display:grid)]:*`, and `[@container_main_(min-width:_32rem)]:*` now map through guarded `@media`, `@supports`, and `@container` wrappers.
- [x] Implement the twenty-fifth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: pseudo-element variants such as `before:*`, `after:*`, `placeholder:*`, `file:*`, `marker:*`, `selection:*`, `first-letter:*`, and `first-line:*` now map through guarded default state tokens.
- [x] Implement the twenty-sixth post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: content utilities such as `content-none`, `content-['hello_world']`, `content-[attr(data-label)]`, and `content-(--dx-label)` now generate normal CSS and compose with `before:*` / `after:*` pseudo-element variants.
- [x] Implement the twenty-seventh post-matrix dx-style parity improvement: `related-crates/style/src/core/engine/parity.rs` now builds a generated-output Tailwind parity receipt from `StyleEngine::css_for_class`, recording supported classes, unsupported gaps, and intentionally different behavior without claiming full Tailwind class parity.

## Current Status

- Live website/template QA: 72/100. The latest live run on `http://127.0.0.1:3001/launch` proves the generated route is real, no longer leaks `{children}`, has 37 `data-dx` markers, serves favicon/static paths, stays no-template-local-`node_modules`, and passes mobile/no-horizontal-overflow and route-smoke checks. Zod/forms, Zustand state, query refresh, local tRPC proof, docs preview, AI route, WASM add proof, automations readiness, and DX Studio markers are visible or interactive. Source has now moved Fumadocs from proof-card language into a dashboard `docs-help-changelog` workflow, including live runtime materializer and conversion-proof manifest selectors for `launch-fumadocs-docs-workflow`; TanStack Query into a `query-backed-dashboard-data` read model; and Drizzle into a selectable SQLite dashboard read-model workflow. The remaining live gap is to re-run Friday-governed runtime QA and continue reducing other proof-card surfaces into product workflows. Several integrations remain honest adapter boundaries: OAuth/Better Auth provider, real Stripe checkout, full Motion parity, strong 3D pixel proof, live Fumadocs renderer, n8n execution, hosted DB/realtime, app-owned wasm-bindgen module, and credential-backed model streaming.
- Supabase `/launch` dashboard package lane: 100/100 for source coding scope. The shell now owns an `account-data-dashboard` section that imports `LaunchSupabaseProfileWorkflow`, while `lib/supabase/profile-workflow.ts` owns public env readiness, editable draft merging, safe fixture data, and upsert receipts with stable Zed selectors. `dx.studio.launch_edit_contract` maps the dashboard section, profile workflow, first-class `supabase-schema-query-workflow` local read-model workflow, and visible `data-dx-supabase-receipt-path` handoff back to their source files; `dx new` carries the concrete Supabase dashboard receipt into `.dx/forge/receipts/2026-05-22-supabase-client-dashboard-workflow.json`; and the receipt now records a `REAL` four-pass audit. Remaining gap: governed browser/runtime proof and real hosted Supabase credentials remain intentionally out of scope for this no-server pass.
- Dev-server hot reload: 94/100. A patched `dx-www dev` served `examples/conversion-proof` once on `http://127.0.0.1:3765/`; editing `pages/index.html` appeared in `/` and `/?qa=1` without manual restart, editing `styles/dx-landing.css` appeared through `/styles/dx-landing.css?v=test`, and `/_dx/hot-reload/version` changed after both edits. Remaining gap: true component-level HMR is still future work; current behavior is reliable full-page refresh with cache-safe route/static invalidation.
- Launch runtime materializer coding pass: 98/100. `tools/launch/materialize-www-template.ts` now source-materializes no-`node_modules` `.html` runtime pages for `/launch`, `/automations`, `/ui`, `/database`, and `/backend`, copies the runtime CSS/JS assets, writes `public/preview-manifest.json` with the embedded `dx.studio.launch_edit_contract` editable-surface and operation contract, disables the conflicting fallback App Router route as `.source-only`, and keeps route-handler exports function-shaped for DX's current handler detector. `dx run --test .\benchmarks\launch-runtime-materializer.test.ts` is green; live server/browser proof remains a separate Friday-governed runtime pass.
- DX-WWW landing page deployment: 100/100 for static landing deployability. `dx-www dev` serves the source-owned `.html` landing on `localhost:3000`, the static export bundle deploys on Vercel with `X-DX-Framework: dx-www`, and the public alias renders the latest editor/platform/local-runtime/agents/automation feature story. The native `.dx/build` output still needed the current Vercel bridge at that checkpoint, so `tools/export-vercel-landing.ts` owned the static export handoff.
- Public DX-WWW launch direction: 89/100 bounded Forge/template launch-ready with an unbounded source score of 93/100 and a live-proof ceiling of 89/100 until governed browser/runtime and live-provider proof are attached. The wedge is clear: React-familiar `.tsx`/`app/` public default, Rust runtime, Forge packages, no hidden dependency surface, `dx-check`, receipts, and fast static deploys. Next step is to simplify public CLI/docs around `dx new`, `dx dev`, `dx add`, `dx check`, `dx forge`, `dx deploy`, and `dx status`, while leaving the many evidence commands as expert/Zed handoff surfaces.
- Launch orchestration docs: 100/100.
- DX-WWW Automations n8n bridge: 100/100 for source-owned connector metadata, auth-schema extraction, visible `/launch` credential schema/workflow-node readiness markers, professional Forge package APIs, real `/launch` release-notification dashboard workflow, starter dashboard readiness/receipt usage, `/automations` route surfaces, and CLI/Zed handoff manifests. Runtime execution remains receipt-only and permission-gated.

- DX-WWW App Router launch route mount: 100/100 for source/generated-template readiness; `examples/template/app/page.tsx` now mounts the composed shell through source-owned next-intl message loading, `dx templates --json` records the `/launch` entrypoint, generated-template file manifest, route contract, stable `.dx/forge/template-readiness/launch-route.json` receipt path, and Forge receipt/doc metadata, and `dx new` writes `app/page.tsx` plus the materialized component import graph, typed materialized-file contract, and receipt evidence.
- DX-WWW full template wiring: 100/100 for the allowed lightweight/source-generated scope; package slices, tRPC API health example, react-markdown content preview, App Router route metadata, CLI discovery metadata, Zed/DX template handoff metadata, launch readiness bundle metadata/artifact, generated-template file manifest/materialization, typed route materialization contract, materialized component import graph, stable template-readiness receipt JSON with summary counts, source-owned Forge receipt/docs, starter UI doc, explicit permission-gated runtime request/status, and the composed source example are wired with source-level guards, focused Cargo readiness checks, and no-execution bundle verification green. Final runtime route verification remains a separate governed pass requiring explicit permission.
